original: A set of bright and colorful hardlight bracelets for each arm. They are lightweight and resistant, providing good protection and improving your look at the same time.
a pair of folding fans with hidden hardlight blades

Hidden inside these intricately decorated and seemingly innocent fans lie a miniaturized hardlight projector that, when unfolded, protrudes a razor-sharp semicircular blade. Equally deadly and beautiful, these weapons are usually associated with wealthy and high class individuals. They can be thrown to attack distant targets.
As you approach the shopkeeper, you hear her let out the galaxy’s heaviest sigh.

“I’ll tell you what I told the last one. I’m not paying anybody protection money. The moment someone fucks with my store, I will find them, flay their skin apart faster than I slice through this chameleon silk, and force-feed them their own entrails while immortalizing my view of the act in a new embroidered rug.” The blue-skinned gabilani glares up with a wicked scowl... that falls apart the moment she sees your face. “Oh, you’re a customer! Sorry. We’re still teaching some of the zaika gangs that nobody does greed like goblins.” She places her current project into a nearby drawer and folds her hands across her petite chest while her robotic body’s forelegs do the same in front of her groin. “Name’s Gwon Kahendrys, the only tailor on Dhaal with a lick of sense.”
“Grand. Now tell me, [pc.name], are you ready to become fabulous?”
That’s a good question. Are you?

Gwon the gabilani cyber-drider puts away her current project when you approach. The blue-skinned tailor flashes a winning smile and cheerfully greets you, “[pc.name]! I’d recognize my favorite customer to be anywhere, and not just because of the co-processor I got installed.” She tucks a stray strand of hair behind a pointed ear and gestures at her wall-obscuring inventory. “I take it you’re ready to make some enhancements to your wardrobe, and you came to just the right place for it.”

original: A blue-skinned gabilani of impressive vertical stature lounges against the shop’s luminous countertop, idly weaving new garments with the assistance of numerous metal limbs from below her waist. She’s a drider! - or a robotic one, at least. Everything from the waist down might as well be made of glossy chrome.
